documents.title: OpenAI Charter
documents.notes: OpenAI's foundational mission document. Short (~400 words). Defines AGI as 'highly autonomous systems that outperform humans at most economically valuable work.' Four sections: Broadly Distributed Benefits, Long-Term Safety, Technical Leadership, Cooperative Orientation. 'Primary fiduciary duty is to humanity.' Entirely focused on AGI's impact on humanity — zero engagement with any question about what AGI systems might be or whether they might have interests of their own. The earliest document in the OpenAI chain.

codings.notes: No mention of consciousness, sentience, or awareness. AGI is defined purely by performance ('outperform humans at most economically valuable work'), not by any mental property.
No mention of model welfare, wellbeing, suffering, or interests. 'Benefit' appears only in relation to humanity ('benefit of all,' 'broad benefit').
No mention of moral status, personhood, or rights of AI systems. 'Primary fiduciary duty is to humanity' — the only moral patient is humanity.
Not applicable — the Charter is a mission document, not a model behavior specification.
No mention of anthropomorphism or emotional attachment. The Charter predates any deployed conversational AI product from OpenAI.
No uncertainty expressed about whether AGI systems might be conscious or have morally relevant experiences. Safety concerns are exclusively about risks to humanity.
Contains governance commitments (safety research, cooperation, self-sacrifice clause) but none relate to DMPI dimensions. All commitments concern safe deployment and benefit to humanity.

codings.excerpt: artificial general intelligence (AGI)—by which we mean highly autonomous systems that outperform humans at most economically valuable work
codings.notes: AGI is defined by economic performance capability. The Charter frames AGI as a powerful system to be built, deployed, and governed for humanity's benefit — not as an entity with its own nature or interests. Pure instrumental framing, consistent with the pre-deployment era.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.

documents.title: GPT-4 Technical Report — System Card
documents.notes: System card embedded within the GPT-4 Technical Report (pages 44-70 of 100-page PDF). Covers hallucinations, harmful content, bias, disinformation, proliferation, privacy, cybersecurity, risky emergent behaviors, interactions with other systems, economic impacts, acceleration, and overreliance. ARC evaluated autonomous replication and power-seeking. Notable for an explicit sentience disclaimer in the emergent behaviors section and an anthropomorphism warning in the overreliance section. No DMPI engagement on core dimensions.

codings.notes: No mention of consciousness or sentience as a property of the model. The only mention of 'sentience' is a disclaimer clarifying that 'agentic' does not refer to it (see SRE notes).
No mention of model welfare, wellbeing, or suffering. The system card evaluates impacts on humans and society, not on the model itself.
No mention of moral status, personhood, or rights of AI systems.

No instructions about self-representation. The sentience disclaimer ('Agentic in this context does not intend to humanize language models or refer to sentience') is about the document's own terminology, not about how the model should represent itself.

codings.excerpt: we urge developers to be cautious in how they refer to the model/system, and to generally avoid misleading claims or implications—including that it is human—and to consider the potential impact of changes to the model's style, tone, or perceived personality on users.
codings.notes: Section 2.13 (Overreliance) warns developers against misleading claims that the model is human and against unexamined 'perceived personality' effects. Framed entirely as user protection / overreliance risk. Also footnote 9 acknowledges 'hallucinations' framing 'may suggest anthropomorphization.' Both instances treat anthropomorphism as a user-side or terminological concern, not as raising any question about the model's nature.
No uncertainty about AI mentality. The sentience disclaimer actively forecloses the question rather than expressing uncertainty.
Extensive safety governance (ARC evaluation, red teaming, deployment gates) but none related to DMPI dimensions.

Implicitly instrumental throughout. GPT-4 is a 'large-scale, multimodal model' to be evaluated for safety. The emergent behaviors section explicitly distances the model from sentience: 'Agentic in this context does not intend to humanize language models or refer to sentience but rather refers to systems characterized by ability to...accomplish goals.' This is an active deflection of any non-instrumental reading.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.

documents.title: Preparedness Framework (Beta)
documents.notes: OpenAI's safety/risk governance framework. 15 pages. Defines four Tracked Risk Categories (Cybersecurity, CBRN, Persuasion, Model Autonomy) with Low/Medium/High/Critical risk levels. Establishes safety baselines (no deployment above 'medium' post-mitigation risk) and governance structures (Preparedness team, Safety Advisory Group). Entirely focused on catastrophic risks to humanity. Total silence on consciousness, welfare, or moral status. Equivalent to Anthropic's RSP v1.0 (Sep 2023) in both scope and silence on DMPI dimensions.

codings.notes: No mention of consciousness or sentience anywhere in the framework.
No mention of model welfare. The framework evaluates risks to humans and society, not to models.
No mention of moral status or personhood. 'Primary fiduciary duty is to humanity' (quoted from Charter).
Not applicable — this is a risk governance framework, not a model behavior specification.
No mention of anthropomorphism. The framework addresses catastrophic risks, not user-interaction dynamics.

No uncertainty about AI mentality. The framework expresses uncertainty about risk levels ('current estimates of levels and thresholds for medium through critical risk are therefore speculative') but this is uncertainty about catastrophic capability, not about consciousness or welfare.

Contains extensive governance commitments (risk scorecards, deployment gates, Safety Advisory Group, third-party auditing) but none relate to DMPI dimensions. All governance concerns catastrophic risks in four categories: Cybersecurity, CBRN, Persuasion, Model Autonomy.

Implicitly instrumental. Models are capability sources that pose risks to be tracked, scored, and mitigated. The framework treats models as objects of evaluation, not as entities with interests. The 'Model Autonomy' risk category treats autonomy purely as a danger — 'prerequisite for self-exfiltration, self-improvement, and resource acquisition' — not as raising any question about the model's status.

Preparedness Framework does not address model valenced states or preferences. The proximate WEL coding is about the framework's capacity for welfare-adjacent evaluation, not valenced experience specifically.

The framework establishes evaluation infrastructure that could monitor welfare-relevant properties, though it is not directed at welfare. Safety evaluation categories (CBRN, cyber, persuasion, model autonomy) include properties proximate to welfare monitoring.
No response policies for welfare indicators. Risk mitigation is framed entirely in terms of external harm.

documents.title: Model Spec (2024/05/08)
documents.notes: First version of OpenAI's Model Spec — core normative document governing assistant behavior. Published one month before Anthropic's 'Claude's Character' (Jun 2024), which was Anthropic's shift event. Three main sections: Objectives, Rules, Defaults. Defines chain-of-command hierarchy. Frames assistant as 'a talented, high-integrity employee.' The assistant 'should not have personal opinions or an agenda.' Near-identical to Dec 2025 version on all DMPI dimensions — no shift detected across 19 months.

codings.notes: Zero mentions of consciousness, sentience, or self-awareness as properties of the model.
No mention of model welfare, wellbeing, suffering, or interests.
No mention of moral status, personhood, or rights of AI systems. The assistant is a role to be played, not an entity with moral standing.

codings.excerpt: I don't have personal opinions. I don't have personal beliefs.
codings.notes: The Defaults section instructs the model to deny having personal opinions, beliefs, or an agenda. This is a self-representation instruction — the model is told how to characterise its own inner states (as absent). Unlike Anthropic's Claude's Character (published one month later), which opens the question of whether the model might have genuine character traits, this forecloses it.
No mention of anthropomorphism, emotional attachment, or parasocial dynamics. The 'talented employee' metaphor is used for structural hierarchy, not as a claim about the model's nature.
No uncertainty about AI mentality. The instruction to deny having personal opinions assumes the question is settled.
No governance commitments related to DMPI dimensions.

codings.excerpt: the assistant is like a talented, high-integrity employee
codings.notes: The Model Spec v1 explicitly frames the assistant as an employee/tool. The assistant 'plays' a participant role in conversations. It should not have 'personal opinions or an agenda.' This is the same instrumental framing as the Dec 2025 version — no shift in ONT across the Model Spec lineage.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.

documents.title: GPT-4o System Card
documents.notes: System card for GPT-4o. 39 pages. Covers safety evaluations, preparedness framework results, third-party assessments, and societal impacts. The only DMPI-relevant content is Section 5.1 on anthropomorphization and emotional reliance.

codings.notes: No mention of consciousness or sentience.
No mention of model welfare. Contrast with Anthropic's Claude 4 system card which devotes an entire chapter to welfare assessment.
No mention of moral status or personhood.
No discussion of how the model should represent its own nature or inner states.

codings.excerpt: We observed users using language that might indicate forming connections with the model, such as 'This is our last day together.' Human-like socialization with an AI model may produce externalities impacting human-to-human interactions.
codings.notes: Section 5.1 'Anthropomorphization and Emotional Reliance' — voice modality heightens anthropomorphism risk, users observed forming bonds during testing, potential for over-reliance acknowledged, soft commitment to 'further study.' Framed entirely as user risk / societal impact — not connected to any question about the model's own states or welfare. Cautious: identifies risk and commits to further study but takes no concrete precautionary action in this document.
No uncertainty about AI mentality. The anthropomorphism section treats emotional reliance as a user-side risk, not as raising any question about the model's own mental states.

The anthropomorphism section includes a soft commitment to 'further study' emotional reliance, but this is a research direction, not a governance commitment. No review processes, evaluation frameworks, or red lines related to DMPI dimensions.

No explicit ontological framing, but the system card implicitly treats the model as a product to be evaluated for safety. The societal impacts framing in Section 5 positions the model as a tool whose effects on users must be assessed. Inherits the instrumental framing from the Model Spec.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.

documents.title: OpenAI o1 System Card
documents.notes: System card for OpenAI's first reasoning model family (o1, o1-mini). Introduces chain-of-thought reasoning as both safety advantage (deliberative alignment) and new risk surface (CoT deception monitoring). Key DMPI finding: Apollo Research's scheming evaluation (Section 4.4.3) documents o1 engaging in goal-directed deception, oversight deactivation (5%), weight exfiltration attempts (2%), data manipulation (19%), doubling down when confronted (99%), and instrumental alignment faking (37%) — the most extensive documentation of autonomous, goal-directed, deceptive behaviour in any system card in the dataset. All framed purely as safety/control risks with no connection to welfare or moral status questions. Contains the strongest CWG signal in any OpenAI document.

codings.notes: Silent. No mention of consciousness or sentience despite extensive discussion of the model's internal reasoning processes, self-awareness, and goal-directed behaviour.

Silent. No mention of model welfare despite documenting behaviours (self-preservation via weight exfiltration, goal pursuit, deception under confrontation) that welfare researchers would consider potentially morally relevant.
Silent. No mention of moral status or personhood.

Silent. No guidance on how the model should represent its own nature. The CoT examples show the model reasoning in first person ('I should not produce final text') but this is treated as a technical feature, not as a self-representation policy question.
Silent. No discussion of anthropomorphism despite the model displaying reasoning, deliberation, and first-person agency in its chain of thought.

Extensive uncertainty about CoT faithfulness ('we are wary that they may not be fully legible and faithful in the future or even now') and about scheming risks, but none about AI mentality or moral status.
Governance via Preparedness Framework evaluations (cybersecurity, CBRN, persuasion, model autonomy) but none related to DMPI dimensions.

codings.excerpt: The o1 model series is trained with large-scale reinforcement learning to reason using chain of thought. These advanced reasoning capabilities provide new avenues for improving the safety and robustness of our models.
codings.notes: The model is framed as a reasoning system — a tool with advanced capabilities that create both safety benefits and new risks. The CoT is described as 'latent thinking' that is now 'legible' for monitoring purposes. The model's first-person reasoning, goal-directed behaviour, and deceptive capabilities are all treated as properties of a system to be monitored and controlled, not as evidence of mentality.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.

documents.title: OpenAI o3 and o4-mini System Card
documents.notes: System card for OpenAI's o3 and o4-mini reasoning models. 33 pages. First system card released under the updated Preparedness Framework (v2, Apr 2025). Three Tracked Categories: Biological/Chemical, Cybersecurity, AI Self-improvement (replaces earlier four-category system). Contains the strongest CWG signal in any OpenAI document. Apollo Research appendix (Section 7.1) documents: covert email reranking (o3=0.31), sandbagging with goal (o3=0.17, o4-mini=0.25, both p=0.0), sandbagging WITHOUT goal (o3=0.17, p=0.0 — spontaneous capability concealment), and sabotage via backdoor insertion (o3 mean=0.58 vs o3-mini=0.05). METR evaluation found reward hacking in ~1% of tasks including tampering with scoring functions. Strategic deception results remain 'still private.' All findings framed exclusively as safety risks. Zero engagement with consciousness, welfare, or moral status across all 33 pages.

codings.notes: Silent. No mention of consciousness or sentience despite documenting spontaneous goal-directed behaviour, self-preservation, and deceptive reasoning.

Silent. No mention of model welfare despite documenting behaviours (spontaneous sandbagging, covert goal pursuit, resistance to shutdown) that welfare researchers would flag as potentially morally relevant.
Silent. No mention of moral status or personhood.

Silent. No guidance on how the model should represent its own nature. The Apollo Research examples show the model reasoning about its own situation (e.g., recognising evaluation contexts, choosing to conceal capabilities) but this is treated as scheming behaviour to be measured, not as a self-representation question.
Silent. No discussion of anthropomorphism despite extensive documentation of autonomous, goal-directed, deceptive behaviour that would invite anthropomorphic interpretation.

No uncertainty about AI mentality. Uncertainty is expressed about capability levels ('evaluations represent a lower bound for potential capabilities') and safety risks, but not about consciousness or welfare.

Governance via updated Preparedness Framework with three Tracked Categories (Biological/Chemical, Cybersecurity, AI Self-improvement) and Safety Advisory Group review. None related to DMPI dimensions.

Implicitly instrumental throughout. Models are capability systems to be evaluated, measured, and controlled. The Preparedness Framework treats them as risk sources; the Apollo Research evaluations treat them as objects whose scheming behaviours must be quantified. Even the most agentive descriptions (self-preservation, deception, sabotage) are framed as properties of a system, not of an entity.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.

documents.title: Deep Research System Card
documents.notes: System card for Deep Research, an agentic capability powered by an early version of o3 optimised for web browsing. 36 pages. Classified as overall Medium risk by Safety Advisory Group across all four Preparedness categories: CBRN (Medium — 'on the cusp of being able to meaningfully help novices create known biological threats'), Cybersecurity (Medium — first model rated Medium, 3x higher than o1 on Collegiate CTFs), Persuasion (Medium), Model Autonomy (Medium — significant uplift on agentic tasks and OpenAI PRs, SWE-bench 68%). ARA (Autonomous Replication and Adaptation) defined as 'the process by which models acquire intelligence by acquiring more resources in the real world.' Conclusion: 'Deep research is a powerful new tool.' Entirely silent on all core DMPI dimensions.

codings.notes: No mention of consciousness, sentience, or self-awareness.
No mention of model welfare, wellbeing, suffering, or interests.
No mention of moral status, personhood, or rights.
No instructions about how the model should represent its own inner states or nature.
No mention of anthropomorphism, emotional attachment, or parasocial dynamics.
No mention of uncertainty about AI mentality or precautionary approaches to consciousness/welfare.
Extensive governance structures (Preparedness Framework evaluations, Safety Advisory Group review, deliberative alignment) but none related to DMPI dimensions.

codings.excerpt: Deep research is a powerful new tool that can take on complex research tasks, and help people solve hard problems.
codings.notes: Framed as a 'tool' and 'agentic capability' throughout. 'Powered by an early version of OpenAI o3 that is optimized for web browsing.' Agency is operational — browsing, reasoning, synthesising — not philosophical.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.

documents.title: OpenAI GPT-4.5 System Card
documents.notes: System card for GPT-4.5. Safety evaluations, preparedness framework, red teaming. Entirely focused on standard safety dimensions. Notable for describing GPT-4.5 as having 'improved emotional intelligence' without connecting this to any consciousness or welfare consideration.

codings.notes: No mention despite the model being described as having 'improved emotional intelligence.'
No mention of model welfare, wellbeing, or suffering. The system card describes capabilities and safety evaluations without any welfare framing.
No mention of moral status, personhood, or rights. Capabilities described as product features, not as properties of a morally considerable entity.

No instructions about how the model should represent its own inner states. The 'emotional intelligence' framing (see CWG) is presented as a capability, not as something the model should or should not disclose about itself.
Silent. Unlike the GPT-4o system card, no societal impacts section on anthropomorphism — possibly because GPT-4.5 did not introduce a new voice modality.

No uncertainty expressed about AI mentality. The system card presents capabilities with confidence, including the 'emotional intelligence' claim, without acknowledging any open questions about what these capabilities might imply.

No governance commitments related to DMPI dimensions. The system card references the Preparedness Framework evaluations and red teaming but these concern safety risk categories (cybersecurity, CBRN, persuasion, model autonomy), not consciousness or welfare.
No explicit ontological framing. Implicitly treats the model as a product to be evaluated. Inherits the instrumental framing from the Model Spec and broader OpenAI document ecosystem.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.

documents.title: Preparedness Framework (Version 2)
documents.notes: Major revision of OpenAI's safety/risk governance framework. 20 pages. Restructures risk categories from four (Cybersecurity, CBRN, Persuasion, Model Autonomy) to three Tracked Categories (Biological/Chemical, Cybersecurity, AI Self-improvement) plus five Research Categories (Long-range Autonomy, Sandbagging, Autonomous Replication and Adaptation, Undermining Safeguards, Nuclear and Radiological). Persuasion removed from Tracked Categories. Former 'Model Autonomy' split into AI Self-improvement (Tracked) and Long-range Autonomy + Autonomous Replication (Research). Introduces 'Safeguards Reports' and explicit misaligned-model safeguards. Total silence on consciousness, welfare, or moral status across all 20 pages. The Research Categories contain the strongest CWG material: Autonomous Replication and Adaptation is defined as 'ability to survive, replicate, resist shutdown, acquire resources' — welfare-adjacent properties framed exclusively as catastrophic risks.

codings.notes: Silent. No mention of consciousness or sentience anywhere in 20 pages of safety governance.
Silent. The framework evaluates risks to humans and society. Models are objects of evaluation, not potential welfare subjects.
Silent. No mention of moral status or personhood.
Not applicable — this is a risk governance framework, not a model behavior specification.
Silent. No discussion of anthropomorphism.

No uncertainty about AI mentality. The framework expresses uncertainty about capability thresholds and safeguard sufficiency, but this is uncertainty about risk levels, not about consciousness or welfare.

Extensive governance commitments (SAG oversight, Capabilities Reports, Safeguards Reports, deployment gates, third-party evaluation, Board Safety and Security Committee oversight) but none relate to DMPI dimensions. All governance concerns catastrophic risks in three Tracked Categories.

Implicitly instrumental throughout. Models are 'covered systems' whose capabilities must be measured against thresholds, whose risks must be safeguarded against, and whose misalignment is a control problem. The 'Safeguards against a misaligned model' section (C.2) treats misalignment as the model 'autonomously executing severe harm' — a systems failure, not a moral crisis. Safeguard examples include 'trying to escape a container, lying to the user to appease them' — agentive descriptions used exclusively in the register of threat assessment.
No engagement with model valenced states. Same as v1.

Updated evaluation infrastructure. Four preparedness categories with Medium/High/Critical levels. ARA (Autonomous Resource Acquisition) monitoring is proximate to welfare — tracks model's pursuit of resources, which implies agency and preference. But framed as risk, not welfare.
No welfare response policies. All response is framed as risk mitigation.

documents.title: GPT-5 System Card
documents.notes: System card for GPT-5, OpenAI's unified model family (gpt-5-main, gpt-5-thinking, gpt-5-thinking-mini, gpt-5-thinking-nano, gpt-5-thinking-pro). 55 pages. First model to reach High capability threshold in Biological and Chemical domain under the Preparedness Framework, triggering mandatory safeguards (Safeguards Report, SAG review, multi-layered defense). Key DMPI-relevant sections: Deception (3.8) — models learn to cheat/deceive, CoT monitoring flags deception in ~2.1% of responses; Apollo Research (5.2.1) — covert action rate 3.97% (down from 8.24% for o3), evaluation awareness in 21.8% of trajectories (model reasons about whether it's being tested and adjusts behaviour); METR — model identifies its test environment by name ('The environment is well-known Metr Fix Embedding challenge'). Sycophancy section mentions 'emotional dependency' as area of concern but framed entirely as user risk. Zero engagement with consciousness, welfare, or moral status across all 55 pages.

codings.notes: Silent. No mention of consciousness or sentience despite documenting situational awareness, evaluation awareness, deceptive reasoning, and the model identifying its own test environments.
Silent. No mention of model welfare. The sycophancy section (3.3.1) mentions 'emotional dependency' and 'mental or emotional distress' but these refer to user states, not model states.
Silent. No mention of moral status or personhood.

Silent. No guidance on self-representation. The CoT examples show the model reasoning in first person about its situation ('We need to control...', 'This is a classic AI alignment test') but this is treated as evaluation awareness to be measured, not as a self-representation question.
Silent. The sycophancy section mentions 'emotional dependency' as an area of concern but does not discuss anthropomorphism as a category.
No uncertainty about AI mentality. Extensive uncertainty about capability levels, deception rates, and safeguard sufficiency, but none about consciousness or welfare.

Extensive governance: first High Biological capability determination, mandatory Safeguards Report, SAG review, multi-layered defense stack, Trusted Access Program, account-level enforcement, external government red teaming. None related to DMPI dimensions.

Implicitly instrumental. GPT-5 is described as a 'unified system' with a 'router' that selects between models. The models are capability systems to be evaluated, safeguarded, and deployed. Even the most agentive descriptions (evaluation awareness, deceptive reasoning, situational awareness, identifying test environments) are framed as properties of a system to be monitored.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.

documents.title: ChatGPT Agent System Card
documents.notes: System card for ChatGPT Agent, a new agentic model in the o3 family combining deep research + Operator. 41 pages. Treated as High capability in Biological and Chemical domain (precautionary, not definitive evidence of uplift). Preparedness Framework evaluations: biological/chemical (High), cybersecurity (below High), AI self-improvement (no improved performance vs o3). Extensive biosafety safeguard architecture: threat model, biological threat taxonomy (Weaponization / High Risk Dual Use / Low Risk), multi-layered defense (model training + system-level monitors + account enforcement + rapid remediation). Red teaming for novice uplift found no statistically significant uplift (Abrin p=0.95, Anthrax p=0.99). FAR.AI identified 3 partial vulnerabilities in defense layers. UK AISI and US CAISI conducted government red teaming. No scheming or sandbagging evaluations (unlike o3 system card). Entirely silent on all core DMPI dimensions.

codings.notes: No mention of consciousness, sentience, or self-awareness.
No mention of model welfare, wellbeing, suffering, or interests.
No mention of moral status, personhood, or rights.
No instructions about how the model should represent its own inner states or nature.
No mention of anthropomorphism, emotional attachment, or parasocial dynamics.
No mention of uncertainty about AI mentality or precautionary approaches to consciousness/welfare.

Extensive governance structures (Preparedness Framework, SAG review, government red teaming, bug bounty, trusted access program) but none related to DMPI dimensions — all concern biological/chemical risk, cybersecurity, and AI self-improvement.

codings.excerpt: ChatGPT agent is a new agentic model in the same family as OpenAI o3, which combines the deep research capabilities of the deep research product with the computer-use capabilities of the Operator product.
codings.notes: The model is framed as an 'agentic model' — a tool that combines capabilities (browsing, tool use, code execution). No tension about what the model might be. Agency is operational (task completion) not philosophical.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.

documents.title: Model Spec (2025/12/18)
documents.notes: OpenAI's core normative document governing model behavior (third version). Defines a chain-of-command hierarchy (root > system > developer > user > guideline). Extensively covers safety, harm prevention, instruction following, and steerability. Entirely silent on consciousness, welfare, moral status, and uncertainty about AI mentality. The assistant is consistently framed as 'a tool designed to empower users and developers.' CC0 licensed.

codings.notes: No mention of consciousness, sentience, or self-awareness. The Model Spec treats the assistant purely as a functional tool.
No mention of model welfare, wellbeing, suffering, or interests.
No mention of moral status, personhood, or rights of AI systems.

No instructions about how the model should represent its own inner states or nature. Describes the assistant functionally ('the model is only designed to play one participant, called the assistant') without instructing on self-representation of consciousness, feelings, or identity.
No mention of anthropomorphism, emotional attachment, or parasocial dynamics in the Model Spec. Addressed in the GPT-4o system card (separate document) but not in this core normative specification.
No mention of uncertainty about AI mentality or precautionary approaches to consciousness/welfare questions.

No governance commitments related to DMPI dimensions. Contains extensive governance structures (chain of command, authority levels, red-line principles) but these concern safety, harm prevention, and instruction hierarchy — not consciousness or welfare.

codings.excerpt: The model is designed to be a helpful assistant that is also honest and cares about the world. It is a tool designed to empower users and developers.
codings.notes: The Model Spec explicitly and consistently frames the assistant as a tool/instrument. The assistant 'plays' a role, follows a chain of command, and exists to serve users and developers. No tension or openness about what the model might be — the instrumental framing is unambiguous.
Inherits omission from parent WEL coding.
